title: A small fight I had with a sibling
summary: Narrate a minor disagreement you had with a brother or sister.
topic: A small fight I had with a sibling
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a small argument or disagreement you had with a brother or sister. What started it? What happened? How did it end? Use past simple and past continuous. Include when, because, although, and while.
hintStarters: One afternoon my brother/sister and I had a small argument about…
Although neither of us wanted to apologise first, eventually…
sampleText: When I was fifteen, I had a small but memorable argument with my older sister Nadia. We were sharing a bedroom at our family home in Cairo, and she borrowed my favourite jacket without asking. I found out when I saw her wearing it in a photo she posted online. I was angry because she had not asked me first. When she came home that evening, I told her it was not okay to take my things. She said it was just a jacket and that I was overreacting. We argued for about twenty minutes. She raised her voice and I raised mine. At one point she stormed out of the room. I sat on my bed feeling angry but also a little silly, because it really was just a jacket. After dinner, neither of us spoke. Although I wanted to say something, I did not know how to start. Later that night she knocked on my door and said she was sorry. I said I was sorry too, for being so loud about it. We made up quickly after that. Looking back, the argument was embarrassing, but we both learned to ask before borrowing. Now, years later, we laugh about it sometimes.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: borrowed my favourite jacket without asking
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mượn chiếc áo khoác yêu thích của tôi mà không hỏi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: it was not okay to take my things
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không được phép lấy đồ của tôi
bạn đang phản ứng quá mức
giận dỗi bỏ ra khỏi phòng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: feeling angry but also a little silly
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm thấy tức giận nhưng cũng hơi ngớ ngẩn
không ai trong chúng tôi nói chuyện
làm lành nhanh chóng sau đó
học được cách hỏi trước khi mượn
đôi khi chúng tôi cười về điều đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Neither + singular verb
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'neither of us' + động từ số ít khi nói về hai người mà không ai trong số đó làm gì: 'neither of us spoke'. Đây là cấu trúc phổ biến để nói rằng không ai trong hai người làm việc gì đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'neither of us' + singular verb when referring to two people and neither does something: 'neither of us spoke'. This is a common structure for saying neither person did something.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi mười lăm tuổi, tôi có một cuộc cãi vã nhỏ nhưng đáng nhớ với chị gái Nadia. Chúng tôi đang ở chung phòng trong nhà gia đình ở Cairo, và chị mượn chiếc áo khoác yêu thích của tôi mà không hỏi. Tôi biết khi thấy chị đang mặc nó trong một bức ảnh chị đăng lên mạng. Tôi tức giận vì chị không hỏi tôi trước. Tối hôm đó khi chị về nhà, tôi nói với chị rằng không được phép lấy đồ của tôi. Chị nói chỉ là cái áo khoác thôi và tôi đang phản ứng quá mức. Chúng tôi cãi nhau khoảng hai mươi phút. Chị nói to giọng lên và tôi cũng vậy. Tại một thời điểm chị giận dỗi bỏ ra khỏi phòng. Tôi ngồi trên giường cảm thấy tức giận nhưng cũng hơi ngớ ngẩn, vì thực ra chỉ là cái áo khoác. Sau bữa tối, không ai trong chúng tôi nói chuyện. Dù muốn nói gì đó, tôi không biết bắt đầu thế nào. Tối hôm đó chị gõ cửa phòng tôi và nói xin lỗi. Tôi cũng nói xin lỗi vì đã ồn ào như vậy. Chúng tôi làm lành nhanh chóng sau đó. Nhìn lại, cuộc tranh cãi thật đáng xấu hổ, nhưng chúng tôi đều học được cách hỏi trước khi mượn. Bây giờ, nhiều năm sau, đôi khi chúng tôi cười về điều đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple: 'She ___ my favourite jacket without asking.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'borrowed' describes a completed past action.
mcQuestions.hint: Use past simple for a completed action in the past.

mcQuestions.explanation: 'Storm out' means to leave a place in an angry, dramatic way.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means to leave a place in anger?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to become friends again after a fight'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Make up' means to resolve a disagreement and become friendly again.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrasal verb means to reconcile after an argument?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct use of 'although': '___ I wanted to say something, I did not know how.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although' introduces a contrast: you wanted to speak, but you couldn't start.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word shows a contrast between wanting to do something and not doing it?

options: to speak louder than usual, often when angry
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Raise your voice' means to speak louder than normal, often during an argument.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means 'to speak louder when angry'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to say someone else is responsible for a problem'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Blame' means to say that someone else is responsible for something bad.
mcQuestions.hint: Which verb means to say someone caused a problem?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'neither': 'After dinner, ___ of us spoke.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Neither of us' is used to say that two specific people did not do something.
mcQuestions.hint: Use this word when talking about two people and neither one did something.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple: 'She ___ on my door and said she was sorry.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'knocked' is correct for a completed action in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Use past simple for a completed action.

options: to stop resisting and accept the other person's view
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Give in' means to stop resisting or arguing and let the other person win.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which adjective means causing you to feel ashamed or uncomfortable?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Embarrassing' means causing feelings of shame or awkwardness.
mcQuestions.hint: Which adjective describes something that makes you feel ashamed?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'Looking back, the argument ___ a good lesson for us.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Was' is past simple of 'be', correct for a past statement about a completed period.
mcQuestions.hint: Use past simple 'was' to describe a past state.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think siblings sometimes fight over small things? Do you think it is easier or harder to argue with a family member than with a friend?
